sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32000223 with Gray Quarter, Inc. (Gray Quarter) for professional services, increasing
the amount by $120,000, for a total not-to-exceed amount of $419,500 through January
31, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the additional managed services is available in the Fiscal Year (FY) 2025
Finance U1 Administration budget. Future expenditures are subject to Council approval
of the proposed citywide budget and Annual Appropriations Ordinances.

sections.current_situation:
The Finance department currently uses the Accela Civic Platform to handle Berkeley
business licenses. This includes a public facing online portal that accepts business
license renewals but does not allow for new business license applications. Finance and
Information Technology are collaborating on an enhancement to the Accela Civic Platform
that will allow all businesses to apply for new licenses through the online portal.
As the City continues to expand its capabilities of the Accela Platform, the configurations
and customizations become more complex. As such, the City requires a higher level of
support and expertise that Gray Quarter can provide. Gray Quarter’s Managed Services
offering provides responsive support to address issues that may arise from either this
enhancement or future improvements. This enhanced support will ensure timely problem
resolution and continued platform improvement.
The enhancement of the Accela Business License module aligns with the City’s Strategic
Plan goal to be a customer-focused organization that provides excellent, timely, easily-
accessible service and information to the community.
sections.background:
Gray Quarter is an Accela Certified Services Partner and Accela Technology Alliance
Partner that specializes in Accela software, with proven experience in many challenging
projects.
Gray Quarter has been providing support to the City through Accela professional services
as a subcontractor since 2018. In 2019, they led the successful implementation of an
online business license renewal portal. As a result, over 25% of businesses are now
renewing their licenses online. As the City continues to expand the portal, we anticipate
further growth in the number of online renewals. This saves customers and the City time
and money, and provides customers with an unofficial copy of their license upon full online
payment.
In 2020, the City established a direct contract with Gray Quarter to assist with Accela
Civic Platform efforts on an as needed basis. Some of the key efforts in which Gray
Quarter has assisted the City – include: 1.) updating an interface file to allow for accurate
reporting of revenue when overpayments are received, 2.) assisting with completion of a
data bridge to create refunds directly from FUND$, 3.) assisting with design and
configuration of ad hoc reports, 4.) ongoing support of the business license module.
In 2021, Gray Quarter helped complete the initiative for Business License Online
Renewals through Accela Citizen Access, making the process more efficient for both
businesses and the City.

Page 3 of 4

In 2022, Gray Quarter completed the following: 1.) developed a new process to handle
delinquent business license renewal notifications, 2.) made changes to accommodate
business license tax rate change for street vendors, 3.) worked with IT to specify new
infrastructure for Accela version 22, 4.) installed new instances of Development and Test
on latest Accela version.
In 2023 and 2024, Gray Quarter has continued to support both Finance and IT by
significa
sections.rationale:
Gray Quarter, Inc. is an Accela-Certified services partner. They have supported the City
in various projects. Gray Quarter has performed specialized process analysis,
configuration, and scripting to support the City's online business license system and
various other IT and Finance projects. As a result, Gray Quarter has in-depth knowledge
of City business processes and data architecture that would take other vendor years to
acquire.
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered completing projects and enhancements, gaining expertise by investing
more time in learning without professional services; however, doing so would lead to an
inefficient allocation of staff resources and significantly extend implementation timelines.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, 510-981-6525
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
Public
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32000223 AMENDMENT: GRAY QUARTER, INC. FOR ACCELA
PROFESSIONAL SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City Council authorized Contract No. 32000223 with Gray Quarter, Inc.
on February 17, 2020 for software professional services; and
WHEREAS, the City Council authorized an amendment to Contract No. 32000223 with
Gray Quarter, Inc. on May 5, 2021 (69,781-N.S) for software professional services to
implement online business license application and renewal; and
WHEREAS, the City Council authorized an amendment to Contract No. 32000223 with
Gray Quarter, Inc. on September 10, 2024 (71,497-N.S) for continued software
professional services; and
WHEREAS, Gray Quarter, Inc. provides design, implementation, and support services to
public sector technology solutions; and
WHEREAS, Gray Quarter is an Accela Certified Services Partner and Accela Technology
Alliance Partner that specializes in Accela software since 2002, with proven experience
in many challenging projects; and
WHEREAS, Gray Quarter has provided online enhancements to the Accela platform that
have become particularly critical in order to keep business license services accessible
and efficient for the community, and this functionality is consistent with the City’s strategic
goal “to be a customer-focused organization that provides excellent, timely and easily
accessible service to the community”; and
WHEREAS, Gray Quarter has provided excellent scripting, configuration and consulting
services for Accela and therefore has an in-depth knowledge of City business processes
and data architecture that would take another vendor years to acquire; and
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to amend Contract No. 32000223 with Gray Quarter, Inc.,
increasing the amount by $120,000, for a total not-to-exceed amount of $419,500, for the
term beginning February 17, 2020 to January 31, 2026.
